channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.description:
Need resurfacing urgently- trip hazard!!

item.title: Dumped vegetation, 3rd April
item.description:
Someone has dumped a large pulled up bush and small tree benches. The bush is blocking the pavement for buggies etc. and the benches on the verge. I thought brown bin lorry might have picked it up but no such luck.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Swanswell Drive, Hatherley, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Missing tarmac around metal covers in road. 90 degree edge exposed., 3rd April
item.description:
Close to the junction of Privet Court and Gretton Road, travelling towards Gotherington

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Gretton Road, Gotherington, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Urgent Maintenance Request: Road Grit and Dust Pollution – Coopers Hill, 3rd April
item.description:
I am writing to you as a resident of Coopers Hill to raise a serious concern regarding the deteriorating state of the lane outside my property, Primrose Cottage. For several years, we have seen a significant increase in grit and debris washing onto the lane from the adjacent car park during heavy rain. As we enter the drier spring weather, this material has turned into a persistent and severe dust problem. The Ownership Dispute I have previously raised this car park with teams from both Gloucestershire Highways and the Gloucestershire Wildlife Trust while they were working locally as it is seriously eroded. However, both parties have claimed that the car park is the responsibility of the other. This stalemate is now having a direct and detrimental impact on our family's health and quality of life. Health and Safety Concerns Whenever a vehicle passes, it kicks up a substantial cloud of fine dust. This is now severely impacting our daily lives: Respiratory Risk: We are deeply concerned about the long-term health implications of breathing in this road grit. This is an urgent worry for my 2-year-old daughter, whose developing lungs are especially vulnerable to airborne pollutants. Restricted Use of Property: We are currently unable to open our front windows or sit in our front garden due to the volume of dust entering our home and lungs. Mud and Slip Hazards: In wet weather, this same grit turns into a thick slurry of mud which is being dragged into our home and cars. Requested Action As the Highways Authority and the neighboring landowner, I am requesting that you coordinate to resolve this. We urgently need: A mechanical sweeping of the lane to remove the accumulated grit. A permanent solution to the car park runoff to prevent this material from migrating onto the public highway. I have attached photos of the current road surface for your reference and look forward to a coordinated response on how this will be resolved

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Green Street, Brockworth, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Man hole cover broken and missing on road, 3rd April
item.description:
On the road directly outside no23 Jasmin Way Reported last week, ref: 70010787 Man hole cover has gone from being cracked to completely broken and fallen within the drain.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 2, Stancombe Grove, Warden Hill, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Potential missing "except cyclists" signs after posts replaced, 3rd April
item.description:
Signs attached to post at junction of Winchcombe Street and Albion Street. There was previously "no left turns except cyclists" signs but now the signs are missing so require a longer round route onto the ringroad.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 37-39, Winchcombe Street, Ewens Farm, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Failed repair again by drain, 3rd April
item.description:
The repair by the drain near the speed bump has failed leaving a sharp edge around the drain. It was only repaired a few weeks ago but very badly. This needs to have more tarmac added around the entire potholes
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 133, Arle Road, Marle Hill, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet
